zh.Zeddam, Bergh, Gelderland, Netherlands: 荷蘭蓋爾德藍德波澤達姆
zh.lifeSummary: Barbara van de Padevoirt於1499年出生在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德波澤達姆。她於1527年與Hendrik van Essen結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1564年在荷蘭享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Barbara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

variants.name: Barbra
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

historicEvents.description: The Netherlands is inherited by the future Habsburg Emperor and King of Spain Charles V.
The Catholic King Charles I of Spain ruled the Netherlands.

en.lifeSummary: Barbara van de Padevoirt was born in 1499, in Zeddam, Bergh, Gelderland, Netherlands. She married Hendrik van Essen in 1527. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1564, in Netherlands, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Barbara van de Padevoirt est née en 1499, à Zeddam, Bergh, Gueldre, Pays-Bas. Elle a épousé Hendrik van Essen en 1527. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1564, à Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Barbara van de Padevoirt wurde 1499 in Zeddam, Bergh, Geldern, Niederlande geboren. Sie hat 1527 Hendrik van Essen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1564 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Niederlande.

it.lifeSummary: Barbara van de Padevoirt è nata il nel 1499 nel Zeddam, Bergh, Gelderland, Paesi Bassi. Lei sposato Hendrik van Essen nel 1527. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1564, nel Paesi Bassi, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.Zeddam, Bergh, Gelderland, Netherlands: オランダジェルダーランドバーグゼッドアム
ja.lifeSummary: Barbara van de Padevoirtは1499年にオランダジェルダーランドバーグゼッドアムで生まれた。1527年にHendrik van Essenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1564年にオランダで65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Barbara van de Padevoirt(은)는 1499년 네덜란드 글델랜드 버그h 즈다에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1527년 Hendrik van Essen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1564년 네덜란드에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Barbara van de Padevoirt nasceu em 1499, em Zeddam, Bergh, Gheldria, Países Baixos. Ela casou-se com Hendrik van Essen em 1527. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1564, em Países Baixos, com 65 anos.

ro.Zeddam, Bergh, Gelderland, Netherlands: Zeddam, Bergh, Gelderland, Ţările de Jos

ru.lifeSummary: Barbara van de Padevoirt родилась в 1499 г. в местности зеддам, бирг, джелдирланд, Нидерланды. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hendrik van Essen в 1527 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1564 г. в местности Нидерланды в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Barbara van de Padevoirt nació en 1499, en Zeddam, Bergh, Gelderland, Países Bajos. Se casó con Hendrik van Essen en 1527. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1564, en Países Bajos, a la edad de 65 años.
